Name: efficient_inference_optimization
Title: Optimizing Language Model Inference on Commodity GPUs through Memory-Efficient Speculative Decoding and Dynamic Workload Distribution
Short Hypothesis: By combining memory-efficient speculative decoding with dynamic workload distribution, we can significantly improve the inference speed of large language models on commodity GPUs while maintaining output quality.
Related Work: Recent works such as Memory-Efficient Speculative Decoding for Large Language Model Inference on Commodity GPUs and CPU-GPU Workload Distribution during Throughput-Oriented LLM Inference on Single-GPU Systems have explored optimizing language model inference on commodity GPUs. However, these approaches focus on either speculative decoding or workload distribution separately. Our proposal combines these two techniques to achieve a more significant speedup.
Abstract: Large language models are computationally expensive due to their autoregressive nature. To accelerate inference, we propose a framework that combines memory-efficient speculative decoding with dynamic workload distribution. Our approach enables practical deployment on resource-constrained hardware by optimizing memory management and distributing computation across all available hardware resources. We evaluate our framework on LLaMA-7B and demonstrate a significant speedup in token generation throughput while maintaining output quality.
Experiments: Evaluate the framework on LLaMA-7B using an NVIDIA RTX A4000 (16 GB) and measure the token generation throughput and output quality.
Compare the results with existing speculative decoding approaches and dynamic workload distribution techniques.
Measure the impact of model quantization and optimized memory management on the framework's performance.
Investigate the effect of different workload distribution strategies on the framework's speedup.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The framework's performance may be limited by the quality of the speculative decoding model.
The dynamic workload distribution strategy may not always lead to optimal performance due to the complexity of the computation graph.
The framework's memory efficiency may be affected by the size of the language model and the available GPU memory.
